{"success": true, "name": "ecommerce-codebase-audit", "description": "Audit, navigate, and iterate on a Next.js e-commerce project (admin panel + storefront). Covers layout patterns, theme system, visual asset inventory/cleanup, image gap analysis, auth cookie debugging, deployment follow-through, and post-deployment client readiness review.", "tags": ["nextjs", "ecommerce", "admin", "audit", "tailwind", "layout", "sidebar", "supabase", "auth", "cloudflare", "org-restructuring", "package-extraction"], "related_skills": [], "content": "--- name: ecommerce-codebase-audit description: "Audit, navigate, and iterate on a Next.js e-commerce project (admin panel + storefront). Covers layout patterns, theme system, visual asset inventory/cleanup, image gap analysis, auth cookie debugging, deployment follow-through, and post-deployment client readiness review." version: 1.6.0 tags: [nextjs, ecommerce, admin, audit, tailwind, layout, sidebar, supabase, auth, cloudflare, org-restructuring, package-extraction] --- # Ecommerce Codebase Audit Inspect and improve a Next.js e-commerce admin panel. Focus on layout consistency, theme application, page structure, image assets, and auth flow. ## Audit Checklist ### Layout Consistency - [ ] AdminShell lives in `app/admin/layout.tsx` (centralized layout), NOT in individual pages - [ ] Run `grep -rn "AdminShell" app/admin/ --include="page.tsx"` — should return zero results - [ ] `AdminShell` includes the sidebar navigation (responsive: hidden on mobile, fixed on desktop) - [ ] Sidebar uses the same links and active-state highlighting across all pages - [ ] All pages render inside `` via the root layout, not per-page wrappers ### Theme System The admin theme uses emerald/zinc on a dark background (#0a0a0b / #0f0f10): ```tsx // Tailwind classes to look for bg-[#0f0f10] // sidebar + card backgrounds bg-zinc-800/60 // borders text-emerald-400 // active text bg-emerald-500/10 // active background text-zinc-400 // inactive text hover:bg-zinc-800/40 // hover state ``` - [ ] All pages use the same color tokens (not hardcoded different colors) - [ ] Active page shows `bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-400` with indicator dot - [ ] Inactive pages use `text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-200 hover:bg-zinc-800/40` - [ ] All interactive elements have `transition-all` for smooth hover states - [ ] Borders use `zinc-800/60` or `zinc-700/60` — never crude `gray-200` or default tailwind borders ### Page Structure Each admin page follows this pattern. IMPORTANT: AdminShell is NOT imported by individual pages — it is applied by app/admin/layout.tsx: ```tsx 'use client' import { useAdminAuth } from "@/components/admin/admin-layout" import { useState, useEffect } from "react" export default function PageName() { const { authed } = useAdminAuth() if (!authed) return null return (
{/* content — no AdminShell wrapper needed */}
) } ``` - [ ] Pages use `{ authed } = useAdminAuth()` for auth guard - [ ] Pages do NOT import AdminShell (layout handles it) - [ ] Pages use `fetch("/api/admin/*")` for data (not direct Supabase client) - [ ] Data fetched via `/api/admin/` routes that use service_role key server-side ### Admin UI Design Standards (El Viajero) When building or redesigning admin data table pages, follow these standards from the El Viajero productos page redesign: #### Table Column Widths Use fixed Tailwind width classes on `` elements so the table fills properly without huddling: | Column Type | Width | Notes | |------------|-------|-------| | Image/thumbnail | `w-12` | Small square, package icon placeholder when missing | | Name | fill | Remaining space, with brand as sub-label | | Price | `w-28` | Font-semibold, optionally show price_before as strikethrough | | Stock | `w-16` | Colored dot indicator (emerald >10, amber >0, red =0) | | Category | `w-28` | Pill badge style | | Actions | `w-40` | Right-aligned icon buttons | #### Action Buttons (NEVER text links) Replace text links like "Editar | Duplicar | Eliminar" with lucide icon buttons: | Action | Icon | Hover Color | Hover BG | |--------|------|-------------|----------| | Edit | `Pencil` | `text-blue-400` | `bg-blue-500/10` | | Duplicate | `Copy` | `text-amber-400` | `bg-amber-500/10` | | Delete | `Trash2` | `text-red-400` | `bg-red-500/10` | Each icon button: `rounded-lg p-1.5` with `opacity-70 group-hover:opacity-100` on the container for subtle reveal. Add `title` attribute for tooltip context. #### Search Bar - Place below the header row, prominent, with a `Search` lucide icon on the left - Max-width `max-w-md` so it doesn't over-extend - Include a clear button (`X` icon) when text is present - Use `rounded-xl` and `bg-zinc-900/50` with `border-zinc-800` - Focus ring: `focus:border-emerald-500/50 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-emerald-500/20` - Filter results locally by name, category, and brand #### Stock Indicator Show stock count with colored dot: ```tsx 10 ? "text-emerald-400" : stock > 0 ? "text-amber-400" : "text-red-400" }`}> 10 ? "bg-emerald-400" : stock > 0 ? "bg-amber-400" : "bg-red-400" }`} /> {stock} ``` #### Pagination Full pagination bar with: - Page range display: `"1-20 de 100 productos"` - Prev/Next buttons with `ChevronLeft`/`ChevronRight` icons, disabled state - Page number buttons with active state (`emerald` bg highlight) - Ellipsis (`...`) for gaps - Each page button: `w-7 h-7 rounded-lg text-xs font-medium` #### Empty State Use `Package` icon (or page-appropriate lucide icon) centered: ```tsx

{search ? "No results" : "No items yet"}

{!search && }
``` #### Toast Notifications Fixed top-right toast for success/error feedback: ```tsx
{type === "success" ? : } {text}
``` #### Image Thumbnails - Fixed size `h-9 w-9 rounded-lg border` - Clickable to open full-image modal - Fallback: `Package` lucide icon on `bg-zinc-800/50` when `image_url` is missing or is `.svg` - Active ring: `hover:ring-2 hover:ring-emerald-500/40` #### Inline Edit Mode When a row enters edit mode: - Replace display elements with `input` elements using `rounded-lg bg-zinc-800` - Use `focus:outline-none focus:border-emerald-500/50` on all inputs - Show Save/Cancel buttons replacing the action icons - Save button: `bg-emerald-600` (green); Cancel: `border-zinc-700/60` (outlined) ### AdminShell Component Located in `@/components/admin/admin-layout` (or wherever it is imported from). The AdminShell provides: - Sidebar navigation with 4 groups: Operaciones, Catálogo, Marketing, Configuración - Mobile responsiveness (hidden sidebar on mobile, toggle with hamburger) - Auth guard via `useAdminAuth` - Consistent background and layout padding The AdminShell is imported by `app/admin/layout.tsx` (the root admin layout) — NOT by individual pages. ### Data Table Loading States Always implement ALL three states: | State | Visual | Implementation | |-------|--------|---------------| | Loading | Skeleton rows with pulse animation | `animate-pulse` on placeholder divs matching cell shape | | Empty | Icon + text + optional CTA | Lucide icon + message + Create button | | Data | Full table with hover states | `group hover:bg-zinc-800/30` on each `` | For the loading skeleton, match the eventual cell content's shape and count: ```tsx {loading && Array.from({ length: 6 }).map((_, i) => (
))} ``` ### Search Bar Design (Detail) ```tsx
{ setSearch(e.target.value); setPage(1) }} placeholder="Search placeholder..." className="w-full rounded-xl border border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-900/50 pl-9 pr-9 py-2.5 text-sm text-white placeholder-zinc-500 focus:outline-none focus:border-emerald-500/50 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-emerald-500/20 transition-all" /> {search && ( )}
``` ### Bulk Operations Panel When a page has bulk operations (e.g. price update), use a collapsible panel: ```tsx {showBulk && (

Title

)} ``` ### Auth Pattern ```tsx 'use client' import { useAdminAuth } from "@/components/admin/admin-layout" const { authed } = useAdminAuth() if (!authed) return null ``` Note: `AdminShell` is NOT imported here — it comes from `app/admin/layout.tsx`. Login stores session in localStorage + elviajero_admin_token cookie. useAdminAuth tries localStorage → Supabase SSR cookie → custom cookie, all via /api/auth with Authorization: Bearer header. ## Product SEO: generateMetadata Pattern Every e-commerce product page needs dynamic `generateMetadata` for search engines and social sharing. The pattern is non-obvious when products are fetched client-side (useEffect from Supabase) rather than server-side. ### The Problem In most Next.js e-commerce apps, product pages are client components that fetch data via `useEffect` from Supabase. This means: - No server-side `` or `<meta>` tags — pages get the default layout metadata - Social shares (WhatsApp, Facebook, Twitter) show generic OG tags, not the product info - Google sees no product-specific title/description ### The Pattern Create a server-side `export async function generateMetadata` in `app/producto/[slug]/page.tsx` that fetches product data independently of the client component: ```tsx import { createClient } from "@ai-whisperers/auth/supabase/server" import type { Metadata } from "next" const staticProducts = c.home?.productCatalog?.products || [] function slugify(s: string) { return s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9áéíóúñü]+/g, "-").replace(/-+$/, "") } export async function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }): Promise<Metadata> { const { slug } = await params let name = "", description = "", imageUrl = "", price = "" // Try DB first — fetch all and match by slugified name try { const supabase = await createClient() const { data: allData } = await supabase.from("ej_products").select("name, description, image_url, price") if (allData) { const match = allData.find((p: any) => slugify(p.name) === slug) if (match) { name = match.name description = match.description?.substring(0, 160) || match.name imageUrl = match.image_url price = match.price } } } catch {} // Fallback to static content JSON if (!name) { const staticProd = staticProducts.find((p: any) => slugify(p.name) === slug) if (staticProd) { name = staticProd.name description = (staticProd.description || staticProd.name).substring(0, 160) imageUrl = staticProd.imageUrl || "" price = staticProd.price } } if (!name) return {} return { title: `${name} | Store Name — Tagline`, description, openGraph: { title, description, url: `https://store.com/producto/${slug}`, type: "product", images: [{ url: imageUrl || "/images/og-default.webp", width: 1200, height: 630, alt: name }], }, twitter: { card: "summary_large_image", title, description, images: [...] }, other: { "product:price:amount": price, "product:price:currency": "PYG" }, } } ``` ### Key Techniques 1. **slugify() matching** — Most small e-commerce DBs don't have a `slug` column. Products are matched by slugifying the `name` field client-side. The server-side metadata function must use the same algorithm. 2. **DB → Static fallback** — Try Supabase first (has the most current data), fall back to static content JSON. Never show a product page without metadata. 3. **Empty return** — If no product found for the slug (e.g., invalid URL), return `{}` — Next.js falls back to root layout metadata cleanly. 4. **`params` is a Promise** — Next.js 15+ requires `await params`. Important for both `generateMetadata` and the default page export. 5. **Price as meta tag** — Add `other: { "product:price:amount", "product:price:currency" }` so social platforms and price comparison tools can extract it. 6. **OG image fallback** — Use `.webp` format in the fallback image path (not `.png`) to save bandwidth. ### Verification ```bash # Check metadata is live curl -s https://store.com/producto/some-product | grep -oP '<title>[^<]+' curl -s https://store.com/producto/some-product | grep -oP ' { const b = new Set() products.forEach((p: any) => { if (p.brand) b.add(p.brand) }) return Array.from(b).sort() }, [products]) // Filter logic if (brandFilter) { result = result.filter((p) => p.brand === brandFilter) } // Dependency array // Add `brandFilter` to useMemo dependency array // UI — insert between Category and Sort dropdowns {brands.length > 0 && (
)} ``` ### Key Points - Brands are extracted dynamically from product data — no hardcoded brand list - Brands dropdown is conditionally rendered: only show when there are brands to filter - The grid layout may need to expand from 5 to 6 columns to fit the brand dropdown - Brands are sorted A-Z for consistent UX ## Auth Flow & Cookie Debugging ### The Core Trap: Two Auth Paths Crossing In Next.js + Supabase e-commerce projects, there are usually TWO auth paths that can conflict: **Path A — Consumer auth (useAuth):** - Login page calls `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` directly from the browser client - Supabase's `@supabase/ssr` browser client sets the session cookie automatically - `/mi-cuenta` reads session via `supabase.auth.getSession()` — works because cookie exists **Path B — Admin auth (useAdminAuth / /api/auth):** - Login page calls `/api/auth` with `action: "login"` - API route creates an SSR client with `createServerClient()`, but often has `setAll() {}` empty - **The session cookie never gets set on the response** — the API returns tokens, but no cookie - Admin layout falls back to localStorage or custom cookie **Common failure modes:** 1. **Login works (gets tokens) but /mi-cuenta shows unauthenticated** — consumer login page fetches `/api/auth` instead of calling Supabase directly. Fix: use `useAuth().login()` (Supabase browser client) which sets the correct session cookie. 2. **Login works but /admin redirects back to /login** — admin layout's `useAdminAuth()` calls `/api/auth?action=me` with a token from localStorage, but the API route's token verification path fails. Fix: ensure the API route can verify tokens against Supabase REST API (not just SSR cookies). ### Fix Checklist - [ ] Consumer login uses Supabase browser client directly (`supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`), NOT a custom `/api/auth` endpoint - [ ] After successful login, store session tokens in `localStorage` for admin layout fallback: `localStorage.setItem("elviajero_admin_session", JSON.stringify({ access_token, refresh_token }))` - [ ] Admin layout checks localStorage first, then Supabase SSR cookie, then custom cookie — triple fallback - [ ] API route `action: "me"` verifies tokens via `fetch(${supabaseUrl}/auth/v1/user)` not `supabase.auth.getUser()` - [ ] Service-role client for profile queries uses `createClient(url, serviceRoleKey)` — passing service key as second arg, not in headers - [ ] Role check queries DB profiles table (not JWT claim) — `profiles.role` not `user.role` ## Supabase Auth Error Translation (LATAM Sites) Supabase returns auth errors in English ("Invalid login credentials", "Email not confirmed", "User already registered"). For Paraguayan/LATAM clients, these must be translated to Spanish in the frontend before display. ### The Pattern In the auth context (e.g. `packages/auth/auth-context.tsx` or `lib/auth.ts`), intercept the error from `signInWithPassword` and map it: ```tsx const errorMap: Record = { "Invalid login credentials": "Credenciales incorrectas", "Email not confirmed": "Email no confirmado. Revisá tu bandeja de entrada.", "User already registered": "Ya existe una cuenta con este email.", "Password should be at least 6 characters": "La contraseña debe tener al menos 6 caracteres.", } try { const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password }) if (error) throw new Error(errorMap[error.message] || error.message) // ... success } catch (err: any) { setError(err.message) // Spanish message } ``` ### Key Points - Map the **exact** English string Supabase returns — check `error.message` in a test first - Keep a fallback to the raw English message for unmapped errors (don't swallow them) - This pattern applies to `signInWithPassword`, `signUp`, `resetPasswordForEmail`, and `updateUser` - Do NOT try to configure Supabase locale — the GoTrue server doesn't respect i18n params; translation must happen client-side ## Product Catalog Migration: Static JSON → Supabase DB When an e-commerce site starts with products hardcoded in `content/es.json` but needs to scale to real client data, migrate the catalog to Supabase and make the frontend read from DB at runtime. ### Migration Steps 1. **Audit existing data** - Check if products already exist in Supabase: `select count(*) from ej_products` - If they do, decide: merge or truncate + re-seed 2. **Normalize categories** Client-provided categories often have inconsistencies. Normalize BEFORE seeding: | Raw Client Input | Normalized Category | |------------------|---------------------| | Pezca / pesca / fishing | Pesca | | electronica / Electronica | Electrónica | | accesorios pers. / Accesorios Personales | Accesorios Personales | | Equipo p/ Auto / accesorios moto | Accesorios para Vehículos | | Camping / campamento | Camping | ```typescript function normalizeCategory(raw: string): string { const map: Record = { "pezca": "Pesca", "pesca": "Pesca", "fishing": "Pesca", "electronica": "Electrónica", "Electronica": "Electrónica", "accesorios pers.": "Accesorios Personales", "equipo p/ auto": "Accesorios para Vehículos", "accesorios moto": "Accesorios para Vehículos", "camping": "Camping", "campamento": "Camping", } return map[raw.trim().toLowerCase()] || raw.trim() } ``` 3. **Truncate and seed** ```bash # Truncate existing products (cascades to order_items if FK exists) psql "$SUPABASE_DB_URL" -c "TRUNCATE TABLE ej_products RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE;" ``` Then run a seed script that inserts the normalized products. See `references/supabase-product-seeding-pattern.md` for a full seed script template. 4. **Update frontend to read from DB** - In the page component (e.g. `app/tienda/page.tsx`), fetch products server-side or client-side from Supabase - Mark the page as dynamic: `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` - The `tienda-content.tsx` component should accept `dbProducts` and merge with any static fallback (static fallback should be empty after migration) 5. **Update content/es.json** - Empty the `home.productCatalog.products` array: `"products": []` - Update `home.productCatalog.categories` to match the normalized category list - This prevents the static JSON from shadowing the DB data 6. **Verify the mapping** - Ensure every product from DB has an `id` field mapped correctly for cart operations: ```tsx const products = dbData.map((p: any) => ({ id: p.id, // CRITICAL: cart needs this name: p.name, price: p.price ?? 0, // ... })) ``` ### Pitfalls - **Missing `id` in mapping** — If the DB→frontend mapping omits `id`, `addItem()` in the cart will fail silently or create items that can't be removed - **Price nulls** — If the client's product list doesn't include prices, all products show "USD 0.00". Insert `price: null` and handle the display with "Consultar precio" instead of "USD 0.00" - **Category drift** — If `content/es.json` categories don't match the seeded categories, the filter UI shows categories with 0 products - **Build-time vs runtime** — If products are fetched at build time (SSG), new products won't appear until rebuild. Use `force-dynamic` or ISR with `revalidate = 60` ## WhatsApp Checkout from Cart For Paraguayan e-commerce sites without payment gateway credentials (Pagopar/Bancard), WhatsApp checkout is the primary conversion channel. Add it to the cart sidebar. ### Implementation In `components/cart-sidebar.tsx` (or equivalent cart drawer): ```tsx const phone = "[REDACTED_PHONE]" // real business number, no placeholders function buildWhatsAppMessage(items: CartItem[], total: number): string { const lines = items.map((item, i) => `${i + 1}. ${item.name} x${item.quantity} — ${formatPrice(item.price * item.quantity)}` ) return encodeURIComponent( `Hola! Quiero hacer un pedido desde la web de El Viajero:\n\n` + lines.join("\n") + `\n\n*Total: ${formatPrice(total)}*\n\n¿Cómo sigue el proceso de pago y envío?` ) } // Inside the cart sidebar JSX, near the checkout button: Pedir por WhatsApp ``` ### Key Points - Use `wa.me` links — they work on mobile and desktop without needing the WhatsApp API - Strip non-digits from the phone number for the `wa.me` URL - Pre-format the message with product list, quantities, and total — the business owner gets a complete order summary - Include a closing question ("¿Cómo sigue el proceso?") to prompt a response - Keep the button prominent (green, full-width) alongside any future payment gateway button - Also add "Consultar por WhatsApp" on individual product pages with a single-product message template ### Per-Product WhatsApp Button ```tsx Consultar por WhatsApp ``` ### Debugging Auth Issues ```bash # Check if API route is reachable curl -sX POST https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/api/auth \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"action":"login","email":"[REDACTED_EMAIL]","password":"test"}' | head -c 200 # Check if session cookie is set curl -sv https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/mi-cuenta 2>&1 | grep -i 'set-cookie' # Check server logs docker service logs elviajero-comercio_web --tail 30 --no-task-ids ``` ## Visual Asset Workflow ### Full Asset Overhaul Pattern When doing a complete visual assets redesign (inventory, generation, deployment): #### Phase 1: Inventory ```bash # List all files on disk find public/images -type f | sort # List all code references grep -rohP '"/images/[^"'"'"']+' --include='*.{tsx,ts,json,css}' | sed 's/^"//' | sort -u # Cross-reference: 3 outcomes per image # 1. Referenced + on disk = OK # 2. Referenced + missing = FIX (add file) # 3. On disk + no reference = ORPHAN (delete) # Size audit — small = programmatic (low quality) find public/images -name "*.png" -size -50k ``` #### Phase 2: Categorization | Type | Size threshold | Keep as | Upgrade? | |------|---------------|---------|----------| | SVGs | varies | Vector | Only if they're emoji text | | Real photos | >50KB | PNG | No | | Programmatic renders | <50KB | PNG | Yes — regenerate with richer Pillow composites | | Icons | 4-8KB | PNG | Intentionally small — minimalist silhouettes | | Hero backgrounds | <20KB | PNG | Yes — add atmosphere, silhouettes, gradients | #### Phase 3: Generation (no AI API available) Use Pillow for progressive enhancement rounds: **Round 1 — Placeholders:** ```python # Sky gradient + bokeh circles + category silhouettes # Write to a generate script, deploy, iterate ``` **Round 2 — Richer details:** - Tree silhouettes, stars, glowing tent, moon - River reflections, fishing rod, sun at horizon - Mountain trails, hiker with backpack - Headlight beams, road lines, car approaching - Dust clouds, motorcycle, fence - Fields, barn, sun, clouds **Round 3 — AI (if REPLICATE_API_TOKEN or equivalent available):** ```python if call_replicate(prompt, w, h, path): print("AI generated") else: render_fallback(path) # keep best Pillow version ``` **Round 4 — Self-critique:** After deploying, review each asset and write a brutal critique doc. Then regenerate the worst offenders in the next round. #### Phase 4: Deployment Pitfalls **Cloudflare cache (IMPORTANT):** - CF sets `cache-control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` — 1 YEAR cache - No CF API key? Only option is **rename the file** (logo.png → logo-v2.png) - Then update ALL code references to point to new filename - Verify with curl, not browser: `curl -sk URL/images/logo-v2.png -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}B"` - Then tell the user to hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) **Docker layer cache:** - If files exist locally but return 404 on deploy, the Docker COPY was cached - Fix: `docker build --no-cache` or touch a file in the COPY layer **Verify the right file is served:** ```bash # Check HTML references curl -sk https://example.com/ | grep -oP '(logo|favicon)-v2' # Check file directly curl -sk https://example.com/images/logo-v2.png -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}B" ``` ### Key Project-Specific Patterns (El Viajero) - Product images: `content/es.json` → `/images/marketing/products/*.png` (34 products) - Category backgrounds (dynamic SVG): `/images/categories/${slug}.svg` — slug values in `app/categoria/*/page.tsx` - Category heroes: heroImage prop → `app/categoria/*/page.tsx` → `/images/marketing/hero-*.png` - Hero carousel: content-driven array in `content/es.json` → `/images/marketing/hero-carousel-*.png` - Testimonials: `"image"` field in `content/es.json` → `/images/testimonials/avatar-*.png` - Logo: `header.tsx` → `/images/logo.png` (or `logo-v2.png` after cache-bust) - Favicon: `layout.tsx` → `/images/favicon.png` (or `favicon-v2.png`) - OG image: `layout.tsx` → `/images/og-viajero.svg` - PWA icons: `manifest.json` → `/images/icon-192.png`, `/images/icon-512.png` ### Generation Recipes **Category icons (128x128):** Create a Python script with `ImageDraw` — tent, fish, umbrella, backpack, car, motorcycle, barn silhouettes on green circle background. These are intentionally small (4-8KB). **Hero banners (1920x600):** Create scene composites: 1. Sky gradient (top to bottom, warm/cool depending on scene) 2. Ground layer (dark rectangle) 3. Scene elements (tree silhouettes, stars, moon, tent, river, fishing rod, mountains, road, headlights, barn, fence, sun) 4. Bokeh overlay (random circles with low alpha) 5. Gaussian blur (radius 0.3-0.5) for polish **Testimonial avatars (200x200):** If no AI available, create colored circles with basic face features (eyes, smile, hair shape, shirt). Tell the client these are placeholders for real customer photos. **PWA icons:** Render from the logo at 192x192 and 512x512 with anti-aliasing. ## Multi-Locale Content Updates (PAB Sites) When updating content across all locales (en/es/nl/de) in a PAB (Paragu-AI Builder) project: ### Safe approach vs. pitfalls 1. **Python JSON manipulation is safer than `patch` tool for large content changes.** The `patch` tool works well for small string replacements, but when replacing JSON blocks spanning 50+ lines, it can match the wrong text, leave orphaned content, and produce valid-but-wrong JSON. This happened when replacing the `processPage` section — patch matched a subset, leaving trailing orphan that was syntactically valid but structurally wrong. 2. **When `patch` goes wrong: stop, don't stack.** If the first patch produces unexpected results (orphan content, duplicated sections, lost keys), do NOT apply more patches on top. Switch immediately to Python `json.load()/json.dump()` — read the broken file, fix the data structure in memory, and write it back clean. Stacking patches compounds the mistakes and makes the file increasingly fragile to further edits. The `processPage` fix required three patches to clean up what one wrong match did. 3. **`patch` can match content from a previously-applied patch** — If a file was already modified, the old_string might not match what's actually in the file. Always read the file fresh before patching, and verify with a direct key inspection after (`python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('f')); print(d.get('targetKey',{}).keys())"`). 4. **Shell variable expansion fails in `execute_code`'s multi-line Python strings** — `for f in $FILES; do python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$f'))"; done` breaks because `$f` isn't expanded inside the Python string. Use explicit file paths, a temp script, or Python's own `glob` module instead. 5. **Always validate JSON structure after content changes, not just syntax.** A file can be valid JSON but structurally wrong (missing keys, wrong step counts, orphan content). After validation: ```bash python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('file'))" # syntax check ``` Also verify structural integrity: ```python d["processPage"]["process"]["steps"] # should have 5 steps d["home"]["services"]["groups"] # should have 3 groups d["aboutPage"]["story"]["paragraphs"] # should have 3 paragraphs ``` 6. **When deleting orphaned content from a botched patch, read the file with JSON parser first**, then find the structural key that contains the orphan data. Delete it from the Python dict and re-write. Don't try to surgically remove lines with patch. 7. **Process step consistency rule:** The same process narrative lives in 6 places across the site. Update ALL of these together in the same script block — missing any one creates inconsistency: - `home.process.steps` (homepage) - `processPage.process.steps` (process page) - `landingInversor.process.steps`, `landingTrust.process.steps`, `landingLifestyle.process.steps`, `landingEmpresa.process.steps` (LinkedIn ad landing pages) 8. **Process for adding new content sections across locales:** - Step 1: Define the content data structure in a Python dict with all localizations - Step 2: Load each locale file, apply transformations, write back - Step 3: Validate JSON syntax for all files - Step 4: Verify structural keys match expectations (count groups, steps, paragraphs) ```python for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]: with open(f"content/{lang}.json") as f: d = json.load(f) d["parentSection"]["newKey"] = translations[lang] with open(f"content/{lang}.json", "w") as f: json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) ``` 9. **Section structure for the 'Our Services' pattern:** ```python data["home"]["services"] = { "eyebrow": "Our Services", "title": "Section title", "groups": [ {"id": "group-id", "title": "Group Name", "subtitle": "Group subtitle.", "items": [{"title": "Service", "description": "Desc.", "icon": "IconName"}]} ] } ``` 10. **Section structure for the 'Story' pattern:** ```python data["aboutPage"]["story"] = { "title": "Our Story", "eyebrow": "Tagline", "paragraphs": ["Para 1.", "Para 2.", "Para 3."] } ``` 11. **Section structure for the 'Process steps' pattern:** ```python data["processPage"]["process"]["steps"] = [ {"number": 1, "title": "Step Name", "description": "Long description.", "icon": "IconName", "image": {"$img": "process.key"}, "duration": "Week 1"} ] ``` 12. **Landing page Calendly URL pattern:** ```python base = "https://calendly.com/nexaparaguay/consulta" utm = f"utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=paid&utm_campaign={campaign}" url = f"{base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-{placement}-{lang}" ``` ## Deployment ### Docker Deployment After modifying content assets (images, content JSON, or static files): 1. **Rebuild** — `npm run build` catches missing images and type errors 2. **Rebuild Docker image** — `docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .` 3. **Deploy** — `docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false` 4. **Verify** — `curl -sI https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/ | head -5` 5. **Force rolling update** — if same image tag, `docker service update --force elviajero-comercio_web` Never assume changes to static content or images are live — Docker rebuild and stack deploy are required. Browser cache is NOT the server — always curl first. ### Production Monitoring After every deploy, add: - Healthcheck endpoint at `/api/health` returning `{status, timestamp, uptime}` - Docker HEALTHCHECK in Dockerfile (every 30s, 40s start grace, 3 retries) - Cron healthcheck via Hermes: every 15m, auto-redeploy on failure - Shell check script: `/root/scripts/check-{project}.sh` ### Docker Build Timeout `docker build` with npm install can take >120s on cold cache. Set terminal timeout to 300s for builds. If it times out, retry — the npm cache is warm on the second run and it will complete faster. #### Next.js `pages/` Folder Conflict (v16+) Next.js 16+ errors with "`pages` and `app` directories should be under the same folder" when a `pages/` data directory exists at project root alongside `src/app/`. Fix: 1. Rename `pages/` to `nexa-pages/` 2. Update all `page.tsx` references from `'pages'` to `'nexa-pages'` 3. Update Dockerfile to copy `nexa-pages/` into the standalone output 4. Run `docker build --no-cache` after rename (Docker layer cache may use old path) #### Docker Standalone + Data Files Next.js `output: "standalone"` produces a `server.js` that does `process.chdir(__dirname)` — so `process.cwd()` points to `.next/standalone/`, not the app root. Data files read at runtime via `readFileSync(path.join(process.cwd(), ...))` must be copied into `.next/standalone/`: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./ COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./.next/standalone/nexa-pages COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./.next/standalone/content ``` Without this, the container runs fine but returns static HTML with fallback data because `readFileSync` silently fails and the catch block returns defaults. #### The "Welcome" Bug (Static SSG Fallback) When a PAB page always renders "Welcome" regardless of content changes, the root cause is in the page component's fallback chain: ```tsx // BROKEN: home.json has 'titleKey' not 'title' — always falls through {homePage.title || content?.title || 'Welcome'} // FIXED: use siteName from the locale JSON {content?.siteName || 'Nexa Paraguay'} ``` Check that `getPages()` and `getContent()` actually read files via `readFileSync`/`readdirSync` at build time. The `catch` block silently swallowing the error is the symptom — verify the files are accessible during the Docker build stage. #### `window.gtag` Type Conflicts When a project uses `declare global { interface Window { gtag?: any } }` and imports from `@ai-whisperers/seo/analytics` (which also declares it), you get: ``` Type error: All declarations of 'gtag' must have identical modifiers. ``` **Fix:** Use `(window as any).gtag` everywhere instead of declaring `interface Window`: ```typescript const w = window as any w.gtag("event", "page_view", data) ``` Apply to all GA4 tracking files: `components/analytics.tsx`, `lib/analytics.ts`. ## Traefik + Docker Swarm Routing Setup When deploying a new Next.js client site to an existing Docker Swarm + Traefik infrastructure (paragu-ai.com VPS at 72.61.44.159): ### 1. DNS Record (Cloudflare) Add an A record in the Cloudflare dashboard for the paragu-ai.com zone: ``` Type: A Name: {site-name} Content: 72.61.44.159 Proxy status: Proxied (orange cloud) ``` ### 2. docker-compose.yml (Traefik Labels) The template's `docker-compose.yml` has superspuma's labels. Update them: ```yaml services: web: build: . image: {site-name}:prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 resources: limits: { memory: 512M, cpus: '0.5' } reservations: { memory: 128M, cpus: '0.1' } labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.rule=Host(`{site-name}.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services.{site-name}.loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" networks: agent-net: external: true ``` Pitfall: the `image` field in docker-compose.yml must match the `docker build -t` tag. ### 3. Deploy (Manual on VPS) ```bash ssh root@72.61.44.159 cd /root/{repo-name} git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) # for @ai-whisperers npm packages npm ci --legacy-peer-deps npm run build docker build -t {site-name}:prod . docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {site-name} ``` After deploy, verify: ```bash curl -sI https://{site-name}.paragu-ai.com/ | grep "HTTP/" ``` ### 4. Docker Build GITHUB_TOKEN Issue The standard Docker multi-stage build (template's Dockerfile) runs `npm install` inside the build container but doesn't have access to the GITHUB_TOKEN for @ai-whisperers GitHub Packages registry. **Two solutions:** **Option A (simple):** Build on the host, then use a production-only Dockerfile that copies the already-built output: ```dockerfile # Dockerfile.prod — used after `npm run build` on host FROM node:20-alpine AS runner WORKDIR /app ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0" RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs COPY --chown=nextjs:nodejs . . USER nextjs EXPOSE 3000 CMD ["node_modules/.bin/next", "start"] ``` ```bash # On VPS: npm run build # host build has GITHUB_TOKEN docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t site:prod . # just ships node_modules + .next docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml site ``` **Option B (Docker build-arg):** Pass the token as a build arg and set it in the .npmrc at build time: ```dockerfile FROM node:20-alpine AS deps WORKDIR /app COPY package.json package-lock.json* .npmrc ./ ARG NODE_AUTH_TOKEN RUN echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${NODE_AUTH_TOKEN}" >> .npmrc && \ npm install --legacy-peer-deps ``` ```bash docker build --build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) -t site:prod . ``` **Pitfall:** Docker BuildKit blocks ARG values from ENV for security ("Do not use ARG or ENV instructions for sensitive data"). Option A is more reliable. ### 5. CI/CD Workflow The reusable ci-cd workflows (Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd) may not be accessible from new repos due to permissions. Example standalone deploy.yml as fallback: ```yaml name: Deploy on: push: branches: [main] jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-node@v4 with: node-version: '20' cache: 'npm' - run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps - run: npm run build - name: Setup SSH run: | mkdir -p ~/.ssh echo "${{ secrets.VPS_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 ssh-keyscan -H ${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }} >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null || true - name: Deploy via SSH run: | ssh ${{ secrets.VPS_USER }}@${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }} \ "cd /root/{repo-name} && git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main && \ export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) && npm ci --legacy-peer-deps && npm run build && \ docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t {site-name}:prod . && \ docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {site-name}" ``` ## Google Maps Browser Navigation for Search-Engine-Blocked Research When search engines (Google, DuckDuckGo, Bing, Brave) block automated queries with CAPTCHAs/IP blocks, use browser_navigate to Google Maps as a workaround: ### Pattern 1. Navigate to Google Maps search URL directly: ```bash https://www.google.com/maps/search/{query}+{location} ``` 2. The browser snapshot shows results as readable `article` elements with: - Company name (clickable) - Rating (stars) - Address - Phone number - Hours - Website link (when available) 3. Click a result to view more details in the side panel. 4. To refine, clear the combobox and type a new query, then press Enter. ### Queries That Work Spanish-language queries work better for Paraguayan businesses: ``` "constructora civil Asuncion Paraguay" "vidrio templado laminado fachada Asuncion" "fachada vidrio aluminio Asuncion Paraguay" "desarrolladora inmobiliaria Asuncion Paraguay" "arquitecto Asuncion Paraguay fachada" ``` ### Limitations - Only shows ~7 results per view (Google Maps limited view without login) - No API access to extract full data programmatically - Phone numbers and emails must be copied manually from snapshots - Results are biased toward Google's ranking, not comprehensive - Works for Paraguay because Google Maps has good coverage there ## B2B Lead-Gen Conversion (e-commerce template → B2B site) When converting the e-commerce template (template-nextjs-client) into a B2B lead-generation site (construction materials, industrial supply, wholesale), follow this pattern: ### What to REMOVE from the template ``` B2C pages to delete: app/tienda/ → product store with cart/checkout app/envio/ → shipping info app/promociones/ → promotions app/producto/[slug]/ → individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) B2C components to delete: search-autocomplete.tsx → no product search for B2B dark-mode-toggle.tsx → B2B stays light theme payment-methods.tsx → no payments on lead-gen site quick-order.tsx → no quick ordering product-card.tsx → replace with spec-card pattern hero-carousel.tsx → use static hero for B2B promo-carousel.tsx → no promos bottom-nav.tsx → not needed for B2B mobile empty-state.tsx → no shopping experience recently-viewed.tsx → no browsing history wishlist.ts / lib/ → no wishlists ``` ### Post-Deployment Content Audit / Roast Pattern After deploying a converted B2B site (or any new client site from template), run a systematic content audit by browsing every page live and checking for: 1. **Template remnants**: Any text/images from the original template site that leaked through (Fun4Me brand, superspuma references, wrong Instagram handles). Check /contacto, /nosotros, /faq, footer especially — these are the most commonly overlooked. 2. **Content that doesn't match the business type**: - B2B construction site still showing e-commerce order tracking - Professional services site saying "bienestar íntimo" (sex shop) - Law firm showing shipping/returns information 3. **Placeholder phone numbers**: Any hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` (Fun4Me's number) that wasn't replaced. 4. **Broken links**: Links pointing to removed pages (/tienda, /envio, /producto/[slug]). 5. **Empty portfolio/projects**: "Coming soon" or "próximamente" on a B2B site that needs trust signals. Replace with a CTA-driven alternative. ### Content Audit / Roast Methodology Browser every page on the live site using browser_navigate and check: | Page | Common Issues | |------|---------------| | /contacto | Fun4Me Instagram, returns/orders/shipping links, wrong phone | | /nosotros | "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, sex shop story, purple heart, /tienda links | | /faq | Generic questions instead of industry-specific technical answers | | /proyectos | "Coming soon" instead of real portfolio | | /productos | Emoji icons instead of real product images | | /blog | No images, missing headers, broken formatting | After audit, fix the 3 worst pages (usually contacto, nosotros, faq) in one commit, then re-deploy and verify. ### Post-Deployment Content Audit / Roast Pattern After deploying a converted B2B site (or any new client site from template), run a systematic content audit by browsing every page live and checking for: 1. **Template remnants**: Any text/images from the original template site that leaked through (Fun4Me brand, superspuma references, wrong Instagram handles). Check /contacto, /nosotros, /faq, footer especially — these are the most commonly overlooked. 2. **Content that doesn't match the business type**: - B2B construction site still showing e-commerce order tracking - Professional services site saying "bienestar íntimo" (sex shop) - Law firm showing shipping/returns information 3. **Placeholder phone numbers**: Any hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` (Fun4Me's number) that wasn't replaced. 4. **Broken links**: Links pointing to removed pages (/tienda, /envio, /producto/[slug]). 5. **Empty portfolio/projects**: "Coming soon" or "próximamente" on a B2B site that needs trust signals. Replace with a CTA-driven alternative. ### Content Audit / Roast Methodology Browser every page on the live site using browser_navigate and check: | Page | Common Issues | |------|---------------| | /contacto | Fun4Me Instagram, returns/orders/shipping links, wrong phone | | /nosotros | "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, sex shop story, purple heart, /tienda links | | /faq | Generic questions instead of industry-specific technical answers | | /proyectos | "Coming soon" instead of real portfolio | | /productos | Emoji icons instead of real product images | | /blog | No images, missing headers, broken formatting | After audit, fix the 3 worst pages (usually contacto, nosotros, faq) in one commit, then re-deploy and verify. ### Full list of Fun4Me artifacts to hunt when converting template When converting the template to a B2B site, these Fun4Me-specific traces survive even after page/component deletion: | File | What to fix | |------|-------------| | `app/contacto/page.tsx` | Hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"`, Instagram `@fun4me_py`, "Estado del pedido" / "Reclamo / devolución" quick links | | `app/nosotros/page.tsx` | `brand.story` fallback to "bienestar íntimo con total privacidad" (sex shop), "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, purple heart emoji, `/tienda` link | | `app/sitemap.ts` | Hardcoded `base = "https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com"`, fun4me page paths | | `content/es.json` | `analytics.ga4` empty string (needs real GA4 ID), `home.hero.ctaPrimaryHref: "/tienda"` | | `layout.tsx` | Theme-color `#1B5E20` (green, not blue B2B), metadata title fallbacks | | `components/` | Any file still importing `@/components/search-autocomplete` or `dark-mode-toggle` | | `docker-compose.yml` | Traefik labels referencing old site name (`superspuma`) | | Blog post `[slug]/page.tsx` | Hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` | After content/es.json fixes, check the about/nosotros pages still reference `brand` keys (which were Fun4Me-specific) instead of `nosotros` keys (which have the B2B content). Fix by rewriting page.tsx to use the correct content section. | Page | Purpose | Content Pattern | |------|---------|-----------------| | /productos | Product catalog with specs | Technical data + "request quote" CTA (no prices) | | /proyectos | Portfolio gallery | Image + building name + scope + year | | /servicios | Service listing | 5-6 service cards with icons | | /cotizar | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product type (multi-select), qty, timeline → WhatsApp | ### Content Model (es.json) Home page sections for B2B: 1. **Hero**: gradient background, headline focusing on B2B value prop, two CTAs (quote form + WhatsApp) 2. **Features grid**: 4-column cards (stock local, certified quality, tech support, payment terms) 3. **Product preview**: 4 cards linking to /productos anchors 4. **Testimonials**: 2-3 B2B client quotes 5. **Final CTA**: Dark gradient, WhatsApp button Product catalog structure: ```json { "products": [ { "id": "product-slug", "name": "Product Name", "description": "Technical description for B2B buyers", "features": ["Feature 1", "Feature 2"], "applications": ["Building type 1", "Building type 2"], "cta": "Request quote for X" } ] } ``` Quote form on /cotizar: collects name, company, email, phone, project type (dropdown), product type (multi-select buttons), approximate qty/m2, timeline, message. On submit → opens WhatsApp with pre-formatted message. ### Theme Use a LIGHT theme for construction/B2B (not dark): ```json { "primary": "#0F62FE", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA" } ``` Remove dark-mode-toggle. The template already supports light mode via content/tokens.json `"theme": "light"`. ### Header/Footer Adaptation Header: replace Fun4Me brand with client name. Replace nav items. Footer: replace brand name, update columns for B2B context. ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Debugging When the template has `@ai-whisperers/*` dependencies with `^0.1.0` ranges that don't match the published registry: 1. Check actual latest versions: ```bash for pkg in @ai-whisperers/ui @ai-whisperers/admin @ai-whisperers/seo; do echo -n "$pkg: "; npm view $pkg version 2>&1 done ``` 2. Published versions differ per package: | Package | Latest | |---------|--------| | @ai-whisperers/ui | 0.4.0 | | @ai-whisperers/admin | 0.2.1 | | @ai-whisperers/seo | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/theme | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/auth | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/commerce | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/whatsapp | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/i18n | 0.2.0 | 3. Set each to its correct version (NOT all to same). The `^0.1.0` range in the template was never updated after the packages reached 0.2.x/0.4.x. 4. Some packages (like `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest`) don't exist — remove from devDependencies. 5. @ai-whisperers/seo 0.2.0 has an empty dist directory — the AnalyticsProvider import fails. Workaround: remove the AnalyticsProvider from layout.tsx and add GA4 via plain `

messages.content:
{"success": true, "name": "systematic-debugging", "description": "4-phase root cause debugging: understand bugs before fixing.", "tags": ["debugging", "troubleshooting", "problem-solving", "root-cause", "investigation"], "related_skills": ["test-driven-development", "writing-plans", "subagent-driven-development"], "content": "---
name: systematic-debugging
description: "4-phase root cause debugging: understand bugs before fixing."
version: 1.1.0
author: Hermes Agent (adapted from obra/superpowers)
license: MIT
platforms: [linux, macos, windows]
metadata:
  hermes:
    tags: [debugging, troubleshooting, problem-solving, root-cause, investigation]
    related_skills: [test-driven-development, writing-plans, subagent-driven-development]
---

# Systematic Debugging

## Overview

Random fixes waste time and create new bugs. Quick patches mask underlying issues.

**Core principle:** ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes. Symptom fixes are failure.

**Violating the letter of this process is violating the spirit of debugging.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST
```

If you haven't completed Phase 1, you cannot propose fixes.

## When to Use

Use for ANY technical issue:
- Test failures
- Bugs in production
- Unexpected behavior
- Performance problems
- Build failures
- Integration issues

**Use this ESPECIALLY when:**
- Under time pressure (emergencies make guessing tempting)
- "Just one quick fix" seems obvious
- You've already tried multiple fixes
- Previous fix didn't work
- You don't fully understand the issue

**Don't skip when:**
- Issue seems simple (simple bugs have root causes too)
- You're in a hurry (rushing guarantees rework)
- Someone wants it fixed NOW (systematic is faster than thrashing)

## The Four Phases

You MUST complete each phase before proceeding to the next.

---

## Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation

**BEFORE attempting ANY fix:**

### 1. Read Error Messages Carefully

- Don't skip past errors or warnings
- They often contain the exact solution
- Read stack traces completely
- Note line numbers, file paths, error codes

**Action:** Use `read_file` on the relevant source files. Use `search_files` to find the error string in the codebase.

### 2. Reproduce Consistently

- Can you trigger it reliably?
- What are the exact steps?
- Does it happen every time?
- If not reproducible → gather more data, don't guess

**Action:** Use the `terminal` tool to run the failing test or trigger the bug:

```bash
# Run specific failing test
pytest tests/test_module.py::test_name -v

# Run with verbose output
pytest tests/test_module.py -v --tb=long
```

### 3. Check Recent Changes

- What changed that could cause this?
- Git diff, recent commits
- New dependencies, config changes

**Action:**

```bash
# Recent commits
git log --oneline -10

# Uncommitted changes
git diff

# Changes in specific file
git log -p --follow src/problematic_file.py | head -100
```

### 4. Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems

**WHEN system has multiple components (API → service → database, CI → build → deploy):**

**BEFORE proposing fixes, add diagnostic instrumentation:**

For EACH component boundary:
- Log what data enters the component
- Log what data exits the component
- Verify environment/config propagation
- Check state at each layer

Run once to gather evidence showing WHERE it breaks.
THEN analyze evidence to identify the failing component.
THEN investigate that specific component.

### 5. Trace Data Flow

**WHEN error is deep in the call stack:**

- Where does the bad value originate?
- What called this function with the bad value?
- Keep tracing upstream until you find the source
- Fix at the source, not at the symptom

**Action:** Use `search_files` to trace references:

```python
# Find where the function is called
search_files("function_name(", path="src/", file_glob="*.py")

# Find where the variable is set
search_files("variable_name\\s*=", path="src/", file_glob="*.py")
```

### Phase 1 Completion Checklist

- [ ] Error messages fully read and understood
- [ ] Issue reproduced consistently
- [ ] Recent changes identified and reviewed
- [ ] Evidence gathered (logs, state, data flow)
- [ ] Problem isolated to specific component/code
- [ ] Root cause hypothesis formed

**STOP:** Do not proceed to Phase 2 until you understand WHY it's happening.

---

## Phase 2: Pattern Analysis

**Find the pattern before fixing:**

### 1. Find Working Examples

- Locate similar working code in the same codebase
- What works that's similar to what's broken?

**Action:** Use `search_files` to find comparable patterns:

```python
search_files("similar_pattern", path="src/", file_glob="*.py")
```

### 2. Compare Against References

- If implementing a pattern, read the reference implementation COMPLETELY
- Don't skim — read every line
- Understand the pattern fully before applying

### 3. Identify Differences

- What's different between working and broken?
- List every difference, however small
- Don't assume "that can't matter"

### 4. Understand Dependencies

- What other components does this need?
- What settings, config, environment?
- What assumptions does it make?

---

## Phase 3: Hypothesis and Testing

**Scientific method:**

### 1. Form a Single Hypothesis

- State clearly: "I think X is the root cause because Y"
- Write it down
- Be specific, not vague

### 2. Test Minimally

- Make the SMALLEST possible change to test the hypothesis
- One variable at a time
- Don't fix multiple things at once

### 3. Verify Before Continuing

- Did it work? → Phase 4
- Didn't work? → Form NEW hypothesis
- DON'T add more fixes on top

### 4. When You Don't Know

- Say "I don't understand X"
- Don't pretend to know
- Ask the user for help
- Research more

---

## Phase 4: Implementation

**Fix the root cause, not the symptom:**

### 1. Create Failing Test Case

- Simplest possible reproduction
- Automated test if possible
- MUST have before fixing
- Use the `test-driven-development` skill

### 2. Implement Single Fix

- Address the root cause identified
- ONE change at a time
- No "while I'm here" improvements
- No bundled refactoring

### 3. Verify Fix

```bash
# Run the specific regression test
pytest tests/test_module.py::test_regression -v

# Run full suite — no regressions
pytest tests/ -q
```

### 4. If Fix Doesn't Work — The Rule of Three

- **STOP.**
- Count: How many fixes have you tried?
- If < 3: Return to Phase 1, re-analyze with new information
- **If ≥ 3: STOP and question the architecture (step 5 below)**
- DON'T attempt Fix #4 without architectural discussion

### 5. If 3+ Fixes Failed: Question Architecture

**Pattern indicating an architectural problem:**
- Each fix reveals new shared state/coupling in a different place
- Fixes require "massive refactoring" to implement
- Each fix creates new symptoms elsewhere

**STOP and question fundamentals:**
- Is this pattern fundamentally sound?
- Are we "sticking with it through sheer inertia"?
- Should we refactor the architecture vs. continue fixing symptoms?

**Discuss with the user before attempting more fixes.**

This is NOT a failed hypothesis — this is a wrong architecture.

---

## Red Flags — STOP and Follow Process

If you catch yourself thinking:
- "Quick fix for now, investigate later"
- "Just try changing X and see if it works"
- "Add multiple changes, run tests"
- "Skip the test, I'll manually verify"
- "It's probably X, let me fix that"
- "I don't fully understand but this might work"
- "Pattern says X but I'll adapt it differently"
- "Here are the main problems: [lists fixes without investigation]"
- Proposing solutions before tracing data flow
- **"One more fix attempt" (when already tried 2+)**
- **Each fix reveals a new problem in a different place**

**ALL of these mean: STOP. Return to Phase 1.**

**If 3+ fixes failed:** Question the architecture (Phase 4 step 5).

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Issue is simple, don't need process" | Simple issues have root causes too. Process is fast for simple bugs. |
| "Emergency, no time for process" | Systematic debugging is FASTER than guess-and-check thrashing. |
| "Just try this first, then investigate" | First fix sets the pattern. Do it right from the start. |
| "I'll write test after confirming fix works" | Untested fixes don't stick. Test first proves it. |
| "Multiple fixes at once saves time" | Can't isolate what worked. Causes new bugs. |
| "Reference too long, I'll adapt the pattern" | Partial understanding guarantees bugs. Read it completely. |
| "I see the problem, let me fix it" | Seeing symptoms ≠ understanding root cause. |
| "One more fix attempt" (after 2+ failures) | 3+ failures = architectural problem. Question the pattern, don't fix again. |

## Quick Reference

| Phase | Key Activities | Success Criteria |
|-------|---------------|------------------|
| **1. Root Cause** | Read errors, reproduce, check changes, gather evidence, trace data flow | Understand WHAT and WHY |
| **2. Pattern** | Find working examples, compare, identify differences | Know what's different |
| **3. Hypothesis** | Form theory, test minimally, one variable at a time | Confirmed or new hypothesis |
| **4. Implementation** | Create regression test, fix root cause, verify | Bug resolved, all tests pass |

## Hermes Agent Integration

### Investigation Tools

Use these Hermes tools during Phase 1:

- **`search_files`** — Find error strings, trace function calls, locate patterns
- **`read_file`** — Read source code with line numbers for precise analysis
- **`terminal`** — Run tests, check git history, reproduce bugs
- **`web_search`/`web_extract`** — Research error messages, library docs

### With delegate_task

For complex multi-component debugging, dispatch investigation subagents:

```python
delegate_task(
    goal="Investigate why [specific test/behavior] fails",
    context="""
    Follow systematic-debugging skill:
    1. Read the error message carefully
    2. Reproduce the issue
    3. Trace the data flow to find root cause
    4. Report findings — do NOT fix yet

    Error: [paste full error]
    File: [path to failing code]
    Test command: [exact command]
    """,
    toolsets=['terminal', 'file']
)
```

### With test-driven-development

When fixing bugs:
1. Write a test that reproduces the bug (RED)
2. Debug systematically to find root cause
3. Fix the root cause (GREEN)
4. The test proves the fix and prevents regression

## Real-World Impact

From debugging sessions:
- Systematic approach: 15-30 minutes to fix
- Random fixes approach: 2-3 hours of thrashing
- First-time fix rate: 95% vs 40%
- New bugs introduced: Near zero vs common

**No shortcuts. No guessing. Systematic always wins.**

messages.content:
{"content": "     1|import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server"
     2|import { createAdminClient } from "@ai-whisperers/auth/supabase/admin"
     3|import { requireAdmin } from "@/lib/auth"
     4|
     5|const ALLOWED_PRODUCT_FIELDS = new Set([
     6|  "name","category","price","price_before","description","brand","specs",
     7|  "stock","weight","image_url","is_new","featured","cost_price","stock_alert_threshold"
     8|])
     9|
    10|function sanitizeProductBody(body: Record<string, any>) {
    11|  const clean: Record<string, any> = {}
    12|  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(body)) {
    13|    if (ALLOWED_PRODUCT_FIELDS.has(k)) clean[k] = v
    14|  }
    15|  return clean
    16|}
    17|
    18|export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {
    19|  const { error: authError } = await requireAdmin(req)
    20|  if (authError) return authError
    21|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    22|  const { searchParams } = new URL(req.url)
    23|  const page = parseInt(searchParams.get("page") || "1")
    24|  const perPage = parseInt(searchParams.get("perPage") || "20")
    25|  const from = (page - 1) * perPage
    26|  const to = from + perPage - 1
    27|
    28|  const { data, error, count } = await supabase
    29|    .from("ej_products")
    30|    .select("*", { count: "exact" })
    31|    .order("name")
    32|    .range(from, to)
    33|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    34|  return NextResponse.json({ data: data ?? [], total: count ?? 0, page, perPage })
    35|}
    36|
    37|export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
    38|  const { error: authError } = await requireAdmin(req)
    39|  if (authError) return authError
    40|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    41|  const body = await req.json()
    42|  const clean = sanitizeProductBody(body)
    43|  const { data, error } = await supabase.from("ej_products").insert(clean).select()
    44|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message, code: error.code }, { status: 500 })
    45|  return NextResponse.json(data?.[0] ?? null)
    46|}
    47|
    48|export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {
    49|  const { error: authError } = await requireAdmin(req)
    50|  if (authError) return authError
    51|  const debug: any = { step: "init", ts: new Date().toISOString() }
    52|  try {
    53|    const supabase = createAdminClient()
    54|    const body = await req.json()
    55|    debug.requestBody = body
    56|    const { id, ...updates } = body
    57|    if (!id) {
    58|      debug.step = "missing-id"
    59|      return NextResponse.json({ error: "id required", debug }, { status: 400 })
    60|    }
    61|    debug.step = "parsed"
    62|    debug.id = id
    63|
    64|    const cleanUpdates = sanitizeProductBody(updates)
    65|    debug.cleanUpdates = cleanUpdates
    66|
    67|    const priceFields = ["price", "cost_price", "price_before"]
    68|    const hasPriceChanges = priceFields.some(f => cleanUpdates[f] !== undefined)
    69|    debug.hasPriceChanges = hasPriceChanges
    70|    let oldValues: Record<string, any> = {}
    71|
    72|    if (hasPriceChanges) {
    73|      debug.step = "fetch-current"
    74|      const { data: current, error: fetchErr } = await supabase
    75|        .from("ej_products")
    76|        .select("price, cost_price, price_before, name")
    77|        .eq("id", id)
    78|        .single()
    79|      debug.fetchResult = { current, fetchErr }
    80|      if (fetchErr) {
    81|        debug.step = "fetch-error"
    82|        return NextResponse.json({ error: fetchErr.message, debug }, { status: 500 })
    83|      }
    84|      if (current) {
    85|        oldValues = current
    86|        debug.oldValues = oldValues
    87|        for (const field of priceFields) {
    88|          const oldVal = (current as any)[field]
    89|          const newVal = cleanUpdates[field]
    90|          debug.step = `compare-${field}`
    91|          debug.compare = { field, oldVal, newVal, oldStr: String(oldVal), newStr: String(newVal), changed: newVal !== undefined && String(newVal) !== String(oldVal) }
    92|          if (newVal !== undefined && String(newVal) !== String(oldVal)) {
    93|            debug.step = `insert-price_history-${field}`
    94|            const insertPayload = {
    95|              product_id: id,
    96|              field,
    97|              old_value: String(oldVal || ""),
    98|              new_value: String(newVal || ""),
    99|            }
   100|            debug.insertPayload = insertPayload
   101|            const { data: histData, error: histErr } = await supabase.from("ej_price_history").insert(insertPayload).select()
   102|            debug.priceHistoryResult = { histData, histErr }
   103|            if (histErr) {
   104|              debug.step = `price_history-error-${field}`
   105|              return NextResponse.json({ error: histErr.message, code: histErr.code, hint: histErr.hint, details: histErr.details, debug }, { status: 500 })
   106|            }
   107|          }
   108|        }
   109|      }
   110|    }
   111|
   112|    cleanUpdates.updated_at = new Date().toISOString()
   113|    debug.step = "update-product"
   114|    debug.updatePayload = cleanUpdates
   115|    const { data, error } = await supabase.from("ej_products").update(cleanUpdates).eq("id", id).select()
   116|    debug.updateResult = { data, error }
   117|    if (error) {
   118|      debug.step = "update-error"
   119|      return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message, code: error.code, hint: error.hint, details: error.details, debug }, { status: 500 })
   120|    }
   121|
   122|    if (hasPriceChanges) {
   123|      const changed = priceFields
   124|        .filter(f => cleanUpdates[f] !== undefined && String(cleanUpdates[f]) !== String(oldValues[f] || ""))
   125|        .map(f => `${f}: ${oldValues[f] || "—"} → ${cleanUpdates[f]}`)
   126|      debug.changedFields = changed
   127|      if (changed.length > 0) {
   128|        debug.step = "insert-activity_log"
   129|        const actPayload = {
   130|          action: "product.price_update",
   131|          entity_type: "product",
   132|          entity_id: String(id),
   133|          summary: `Precio actualizado: ${data?.[0]?.name || "producto #" + id}`,
   134|          details: { changes: changed, old_values: oldValues, new_values: cleanUpdates },
   135|        }
   136|        debug.activityPayload = actPayload
   137|        const { data: actData, error: actErr } = await supabase.from("ej_activity_log").insert(actPayload).select()
   138|        debug.activityResult = { actData, actErr }
   139|        if (actErr) {
   140|          debug.step = "activity_log-error"
   141|          return NextResponse.json({ error: actErr.message, code: actErr.code, hint: actErr.hint, details: actErr.details, debug }, { status: 500 })
   142|        }
   143|      }
   144|    }
   145|
   146|    debug.step = "done"
   147|    return NextResponse.json({ data: data?.[0] ?? null })
   148|  } catch (err: any) {
   149|    debug.step = "uncaught-exception"
   150|    debug.errorName = err?.name
   151|    debug.errorMessage = err?.message
   152|    debug.errorStack = err?.stack
   153|    debug.errorCode = err?.code
   154|    console.error("[PATCH /api/admin/products] uncaught error:", err)
   155|    return NextResponse.json({ error: err?.message || "Internal server error", debug }, { status: 500 })
   156|  }
   157|}
   158|
   159|export async function DELETE(req: NextRequest) {
   160|  const { error: authError } = await requireAdmin(req)
   161|  if (authError) return authError
   162|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
   163|  const { searchParams } = new URL(req.url)
   164|  const id = searchParams.get("id")
   165|  if (!id) return NextResponse.json({ error: "id required" }, { status: 400 })
   166|  const { error } = await supabase.from("ej_products").delete().eq("id", id)
   167|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })

[Subdirectory context discovered: ~/elviajero/CLAUDE.md]
# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Local:** /root/elviajero
- **Docker service:** elviajero_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`) || Host(`viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter. Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) — shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`
- Customer auth: Supabase Auth (email/password, Google, Facebook)
- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero --with-registry-auth
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

{"content": "     1|"use client"
     2|
     3|import {
     4|  createContext,
     5|  useContext,
     6|  useState,
     7|  useEffect,
     8|  useCallback,
     9|  type ReactNode,
    10|} from "react"
    11|import { createBrowserClient } from "@supabase/ssr"
    12|import type { User } from "./types"
    13|
    14|const supabase = createBrowserClient(
    15|  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    16|  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
    17|)
    18|
    19|interface AuthCtx {
    20|  user: User | null
    21|  orders: any[]
    22|  addresses: any[]
    23|  favorites: string[]
    24|  login: (email: string, password: [REDACTED] => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    25|  loginWithGoogle: () => Promise<void>
    26|  loginWithFacebook: () => Promise<void>
    27|  register: (name: string, email: string, password: [REDACTED] phone: string) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    28|  logout: () => Promise<void>
    29|  updateProfile: (data: Partial<User>) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    30|  changePassword: [REDACTED] string, newPass: string) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    31|  addAddress: (addr: any) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    32|  updateAddress: (id: string, addr: any) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    33|  removeAddress: (id: string) => Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
    34|  toggleFavorite: (productName: string) => void
    35|}
    36|
    37|const AuthContext = createContext<AuthCtx>(null!)
    38|
    39|function loadFromStorage<T>(key: string, fallback: T): T {
    40|  if (typeof window === "undefined") return fallback
    41|  try {
    42|    const v = localStorage.getItem(key)
    43|    return v ? JSON.parse(v) : fallback
    44|  } catch {
    45|    return fallback
    46|  }
    47|}
    48|
    49|function saveToStorage(key: string, value: any) {
    50|  if (typeof window === "undefined") return
    51|  localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(value))
    52|}
    53|
    54|export function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
    55|  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null)
    56|  const [addresses, setAddresses] = useState<any[]>([])
    57|  const [favorites, setFavorites] = useState<string[]>([])
    58|  const [orders, setOrders] = useState<any[]>([])
    59|  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    60|
    61|  useEffect(() => {
    62|    const storedUser = loadFromStorage<User | null>("viajero_user", null)
    63|    if (storedUser) setUser(storedUser)
    64|    setAddresses(loadFromStorage<any[]>("viajero_addresses_" + storedUser?.id, []))
    65|    setFavorites(loadFromStorage<string[]>("viajero_favorites", []))
    66|    setOrders(loadFromStorage<any[]>("viajero_orders_" + storedUser?.id, []))
    67|    setLoading(false)
    68|
    69|    // Listen for auth state
    70|    const { data: { subscription } } = supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange(async (event, session) => {
    71|      if (session?.user) {
    72|        const u: User = {
    73|          id: session.user.id,
    74|          email: session.user.email || "",
    75|          name: session.user.user_metadata?.name || session.user.email?.split("@")[0] || "",
    76|          phone: session.user.user_metadata?.phone || "",
    77|          role: "customer",
    78|          createdAt: session.user.created_at || "",
    79|        }
    80|        setUser(u)
    81|        saveToStorage("viajero_user", u)
    82|        setAddresses(loadFromStorage<any[]>("viajero_addresses_" + u.id, []))
    83|        setOrders(loadFromStorage<any[]>("viajero_orders_" + u.id, []))
    84|      } else {
    85|        setUser(null)
    86|        localStorage.removeItem("viajero_user")
    87|      }
    88|    })
    89|
    90|    return () => subscription.unsubscribe()
    91|  }, [])
    92|
    93|  const login = useCallback(async (email: string, password: [REDACTED] => {
    94|    try {
    95|      const res = await fetch('/api/auth', {
    96|        method: 'POST',
    97|        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    98|        body: JSON.stringify({ action: 'login', email, password }),
    99|      })
   100|      const data = await res.json()
   101|      if (!data.ok) {
   102|        return { ok: false, error: data.error || 'Error al iniciar sesión' }
   103|      }
   104|      // After successful login, fetch user session from server
   105|      const meRes = await fetch('/api/auth', {
   106|        method: 'POST',
   107|        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   108|        body: JSON.stringify({ action: 'me' }),
   109|      })
   110|      const meData = await meRes.json()
   111|      if (!meData.ok || !meData.user) {
   112|        return { ok: false, error: 'No se pudo obtener la sesión' }
   113|      }
   114|      const u: User = meData.user
   115|      setUser(u)
   116|      saveToStorage("viajero_user", u)
   117|      setAddresses(loadFromStorage<any[]>("viajero_addresses_" + u.id, []))
   118|      setOrders(loadFromStorage<any[]>("viajero_orders_" + u.id, []))
   119|      return { ok: true }
   120|    } catch (e: any) {
   121|      return { ok: false, error: e.message || 'Error de conexión' }
   122|    }
   123|  }, [])
   124|
   125|  const loginWithGoogle = useCallback(async () => {
   126|    await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: "google" })
   127|  }, [])
   128|
   129|  const loginWithFacebook = useCallback(async () => {
   130|    await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: "facebook" })
   131|  }, [])
   132|
   133|  const register = useCallback(async (name: string, email: string, password: [REDACTED] phone: string) => {
   134|    const { error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
   135|      email,
   136|      password,
   137|      options: { data: { name, phone } },
   138|    })
   139|    if (error) return { ok: false, error: error.message }
   140|    return { ok: true }
   141|  }, [])
   142|
   143|  const logout = useCallback(async () => {
   144|    await supabase.auth.signOut()
   145|    setUser(null)
   146|    setOrders([])
   147|    setAddresses([])
   148|    localStorage.removeItem("viajero_user")
   149|  }, [])
   150|
   151|  const updateProfile = useCallback(async (data: Partial<User>) => {
   152|    try {
   153|      const { error } = await supabase.auth.updateUser({
   154|        data: { name: data.name, phone: data.phone },
   155|      })
   156|      if (error) return { ok: false, error: error.message }
   157|      if (user) {
   158|        const updated = { ...user, ...data }
   159|        setUser(updated)
   160|        saveToStorage("viajero_user", updated)
   161|      }
   162|      return { ok: true }
   163|    } catch (e: any) {
   164|      return { ok: false, error: e.message }
   165|    }
   166|  }, [user])
   167|
   168|  const changePassword: [REDACTED] (_current: string, newPass: string) => {
   169|    const { error } = await supabase.auth.updateUser({ password: [REDACTED] })
   170|    if (error) return { ok: false, error: error.message }
   171|    return { ok: true }
   172|  }, [])
   173|
   174|  const addAddress = useCallback(async (addr: any) => {
   175|    const newAddr = { ...addr, id: crypto.randomUUID?.() || Math.random().toString(36).slice(2) }
   176|    const updated = [...addresses, newAddr]
   177|    setAddresses(updated)
   178|    saveToStorage("viajero_addresses_" + user?.id, updated)
   179|    return { ok: true }
   180|  }, [addresses, user])
   181|
   182|  const updateAddress = useCallback(async (id: string, addr: any) => {

function.arguments:
{"code":"from pathlib import Path
p=Path('/root/elviajero/app/admin/productos/page.tsx')
s=p.read_text()
insert='''\nfunction adminHeaders(extra: Record<string, string> = {}) {\n  if (typeof window === "undefined") return extra\n  try {\n    const stored = localStorage.getItem("elviajero_admin_session")\n    if (!stored) return extra\n    const session = JSON.parse(stored)\n    const accessToken = session?.access_token || session\n    return accessToken ? { ...extra, Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}` } : extra\n  } catch {\n    return extra\n  }\n}\n\nfunction adminFetch(input: RequestInfo | URL, init: RequestInit = {}) {\n  const baseHeaders = init.headers instanceof Headers\n    ? Object.fromEntries(init.headers.entries())\n    : Array.isArray(init.headers)\n      ? Object.fromEntries(init.headers)\n      : (init.headers as Record<string, string> | undefined) || {}\n  return fetch(input, { ...init, headers: adminHeaders(baseHeaders) })\n}\n'''
needle='''function calcMargin(price: string, cost: string) {\n  const p = parsePrice(price)\n  const c = parsePrice(cost)\n  if (!p || !c) return null\n  return Math.round(((p - c) / p) * 100)\n}\n'''
if 'function adminHeaders' not in s:
    s=s.replace(needle, needle+insert)
# replace all admin API fetch calls in this file
s=s.replace('fetch(`/api/admin/products?page=${page}&perPage=${PER_PAGE}`)', 'adminFetch(`/api/admin/products?page=${page}&perPage=${PER_PAGE}`)')
s=s.replace('fetch("/api/admin/categories")', 'adminFetch("/api/admin/categories")')
s=s.replace('fetch("/api/admin/products", {', 'adminFetch("/api/admin/products", {')
s=s.replace('fetch("/api/admin/products?id=" + id, { method: "DELETE" })', 'adminFetch("/api/admin/products?id=" + id, { method: "DELETE" })')
s=s.replace('window.open("/api/admin/products/export", "_blank")', 'window.open("/api/admin/products/export", "_blank")')
s=s.replace('fetch("/api/admin/products/import", { method: "POST", body: formData })', 'adminFetch("/api/admin/products/import", { method: "POST", body: formData })')
s=s.replace('fetch("/api/admin/stock-movements", {', 'adminFetch("/api/admin/stock-movements", {')
s=s.replace('fetch(`/api/admin/products/price-history?product_id=${product.id}`)', 'adminFetch(`/api/admin/products/price-history?product_id=${product.id}`)')
p.write_text(s)
print('patched product admin fetches')"}
